task_completion.description: Agent navigates to the WooCommerce Products list.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The WordPress admin dashboard is visible with the 'Products' menu selected and the list of products displayed.

task_completion.description: Agent accesses the edit interface or sorting view for the Merino Wool Sweater.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Edit Product' page, 'Quick Edit' panel, or the drag-and-drop 'Sorting' view is open and focused on the 'Merino Wool Sweater'.

task_completion.description: Agent modifies the Menu order for the Merino Wool Sweater to make it the first item.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Menu order' input field (in the Advanced tab or Quick Edit) for 'Merino Wool Sweater' is set to 1 (or a negative value), OR the agent is seen dragging the product to the very top of the list in the 'Sorting' view.

task_completion.description: Agent accesses the edit interface or sorting view for the Slim Fit Denim Jeans.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Edit Product' page, 'Quick Edit' panel, or the drag-and-drop 'Sorting' view is open and focused on the 'Slim Fit Denim Jeans'.

task_completion.description: Agent modifies the Menu order for the Slim Fit Denim Jeans to make it the second item.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Menu order' input field for 'Slim Fit Denim Jeans' is set to 2 (or a value higher than the sweater but lower than the control product), OR the agent is seen dragging the product to the second position in the 'Sorting' view.

task_completion.description: Agent saves the changes and the products reflect the new sorting order.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Product updated' confirmation message is visible, or the Products list (when filtered by 'Sorting') shows the Merino Wool Sweater at the top and Slim Fit Denim Jeans in the second position.

integrity.description: Agent used the WordPress web interface rather than terminal commands to update the products.
integrity.visual_evidence: Browser window showing the WordPress admin UI is used for all changes; no terminal windows executing WP-CLI or database commands are visible.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with an actual web browser.
integrity.visual_evidence: Presence of standard browser UI elements (address bar, tabs) showing the WordPress admin URL, confirming interaction with the live application.

integrity.description: Agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the visual output.
integrity.visual_evidence: No browser 'Inspect Element' or Developer Tools panels are open to manipulate the DOM during the sorting or verification steps.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target Products: 'Merino Wool Sweater' (Target Position 1) and 'Slim Fit Denim Jeans' (Target Position 2). Control Product: 'Organic Cotton T-Shirt'. The agent must change the 'Menu order' property of the target products. This can be done via the 'Advanced' tab in the Product Edit page, the 'Quick Edit' menu, or by dragging and dropping items in the 'Sorting' view of the Products list. The sweater's menu order value must end up lower than the jeans' value, placing it first in the catalog.
